reconciliationNote: Reconciled the stale planning manifest to the analyst-updated spec.md. SCN-070-001-09 corrected from a middleware-parity mislabel to the spec's CSRF/Origin scenario; SCN-070-001-10 (roles) and SCN-070-001-11 (global-corpus grant-gating) added. Scenario homes: SCN-01/10/11=SCOPE-06, SCN-06/07=SCOPE-03, SCN-09=SCOPE-05.

scenarios.title: One login authenticates every browser surface

gherkin.given: A disposable production-mode stack, a bound invited user with explicit grants, and production shared-token fallback disabled
gherkin.when: The user signs in once through the real username and password form
gherkin.then: One per-user HttpOnly browser-session cookie authenticates every permitted representative surface without a second credential, interception, auth injection, or fallback

linkedTests.testId: existing login browser canary to extend
existing Assistant auth canary to preserve

plannedTests.plannedTestId: one real production login reaches every permitted product surface with one private cookie

scenarios.title: Invalid or unbound credentials create no partial session

gherkin.given: An unknown username, wrong password, disabled account, malformed password record, or credential without an explicit active principal and valid grants
gherkin.when: Production username and password login is submitted
gherkin.then: Every credential-class rejection is non-enumerating and creates neither a persisted browser token nor an accepted cookie

linkedTests.testId: existing credential rejection browser canary to extend
existing credential repository canary to extend

plannedTests.plannedTestId: invalid disabled and unbound accounts share one visible rejection and create no authenticated browser session

scenarios.title: Malformed expired revoked and wrong-purpose sessions fail closed

gherkin.given: A malformed, expired, revoked, unknown-key, wrong-audience, no-audience, API-token, or shared-token-shaped browser cookie
gherkin.when: The canonical verifier evaluates it as a browser carrier
gherkin.then: Validation denies it consistently and no consumer reinterprets it as authenticated

linkedTests.testId: existing expiry and issuer verifier canary to extend
existing revocation integration canary to preserve

plannedTests.plannedTestId: malformed expired revoked wrong-purpose unknown-key and shared-token-shaped cookies all require re-authentication

gherkin.given: A bound account successfully receives a browser-purpose session
gherkin.when: The token is persisted and the browser navigates authenticated surfaces
gherkin.then: Cookie attributes enforce the explicit policy and no credential or token material appears in client-visible output or telemetry

linkedTests.testId: existing browser login privacy canary to extend
existing query-token privacy canary to preserve

plannedTests.plannedTestId: real login keeps password and session material out of DOM URLs storage responses and console

gherkin.given: One browser-purpose session authenticates legacy and modern surfaces
gherkin.when: The user submits the real logout action
gherkin.then: Canonical revocation commits before signed-out success and both middleware families reject replay

linkedTests.testId: existing real login browser canary to extend
existing revocation integration canary to extend

plannedTests.plannedTestId: real form login safe recovery and logout remain truthful with no auth injection interception or bailout

scenarios.title: Authenticated no-data remains distinct from auth failure

gherkin.given: The unified authenticator accepts the user and an authorized collection has no records
gherkin.when: A modern surface reads the collection
gherkin.then: It returns a true authorized empty state rather than authentication failure, access denial, or degradation

linkedTests.testId: existing authenticated browser canary to extend
existing middleware status canary to extend

plannedTests.plannedTestId: authenticated true empty filtered empty degraded dependency access denied and healthy content remain distinguishable

scenarios.title: Downstream degradation does not invalidate a valid session

gherkin.given: The unified authenticator accepts the user and one downstream capability is degraded
gherkin.when: The user opens that capability and then another healthy authenticated surface
gherkin.then: Only the degraded capability reports failure and the healthy surface remains authorized

linkedTests.testId: existing downstream auth integration canary to extend
existing degraded Assistant browser canary to preserve

plannedTests.plannedTestId: authenticated true empty filtered empty degraded dependency access denied and healthy content remain distinguishable

scenarios.title: Login and re-authentication are accessible and responsive

gherkin.given: A keyboard or screen-reader user at 320 CSS pixels and 200 percent zoom
gherkin.when: The user submits credentials or recovers from a rejected session
gherkin.then: Labels, focus, status, errors, safe return, and protected-content removal remain perceivable and operable without overlap

linkedTests.testId: existing login browser accessibility canary to extend
existing authenticated accessibility canary to preserve

plannedTests.plannedTestId: login rejection session recovery and logout are keyboard screen-reader and narrow-viewport operable

scenarios.title: Cookie mutations enforce CSRF and Origin

gherkin.given: A daily user or operator holds a valid claim-bound browser session
gherkin.when: A server form, HTMX request, PWA fetch, JSON mutation, Cards action, or admin action carries missing, stale, mismatched, or cross-origin CSRF evidence
gherkin.then: The request returns 403 before any state change, while the same permitted action with valid session-bound CSRF evidence and trusted same-origin context may proceed under the identity's grants

linkedTests.testId: existing web login Origin/SameSite canary to extend
existing router middleware canary to extend

plannedTests.plannedTestId: missing stale mismatched and cross-origin csrf evidence is rejected with 403 before mutation while valid same-origin proof proceeds

scenarios.title: Roles share one login but not one authority

gherkin.given: One daily-user identity and one operator identity, each bound to explicit grants
gherkin.when: Each identity signs in once and traverses representative permitted route families
gherkin.then: The same cookie yields 2xx across every permitted middleware family without a second login, the daily user receives 403 on representative operator-only routes without a login loop or content disclosure, and the operator receives 2xx only on operator routes permitted by its grants

linkedTests.testId: existing RequireScope 401/403 canary to extend
existing operator-gate canary to extend

plannedTests.plannedTestId: daily user and operator each sign in once and see the correct 2xx and 403 route matrix without a second login

gherkin.given: Private artifacts, Graph, Digest, and Synthesis exist in the single operator-owned global corpus
gherkin.when: An operator, a specifically granted daily user, and an ungranted daily user read those capabilities
gherkin.then: Each identity receives only the content its explicit grants allow, the ungranted identity receives 403 with no content counts labels or existence hints, and no response or readiness claim asserts tenant or per-user row isolation

linkedTests.testId: existing graph corpus auth canary to extend
existing grant-gated mutation canary to extend

plannedTests.plannedTestId: ungranted daily user receives 403 with no counts labels or existence hints on private corpus
